sideA.text: quantity, unit_price, and total are each authoritative — copy each exactly from the invoice.

sideB.text: But DO infer quantity/unit when clearly present inside product names.

contradictions.conflict: Same field (quantity) has dual authority: column copy vs name inference. No precedence rule when they disagree.
vlEvidence: Bocconcino POMODOR: column qty=2 vs inferred *6→6
Mammafiore Aceto: column qty=1 vs inferred *2→2
Emporio Ginger Beer: column qty=2 vs inferred pack→24

sideB.text: DO infer quantity/unit when clearly present inside product names. / "Salada" with unit "EM" → quantity: 1

contradictions.conflict: Inference from partial name signals (unit only, pack *N) is indistinguishable from invention when column value differs or is ambiguous.
vlEvidence: Mammafiore phantom Olio — invented row from fused cues
Salada EM example teaches default qty=1 without column evidence

sideB.text: Screenshots may have messy OCR. Use contextual reasoning.

contradictions.conflict: Contextual reasoning on messy OCR encourages reconstructing values not visibly present — opposite of anti-hallucination.
vlEvidence: Mammafiore phantom row — contextual fusion of lot 609 + 5l*2 + numerics

sideA.text: If quantity truly cannot be determined: quantity = null, unit = null

sideB.text: Seven worked examples all produce non-null qty/unit from name patterns; Manjericão/Salada infer without visible qty

contradictions.conflict: Examples overwhelmingly teach inference; null escape hatch is single line with no example. Model prefers guessing.
vlEvidence: Aceto/Rulo raw runs: qty=2 from *2 rather than null or column=1
GPT rarely emits null in VL corpus per instruction-inventory audit

sideA.text: quantity must represent the PURCHASED quantity. / purchased quantity = pack/case/unit count, NOT per-item weight

sideB.text: Pack examples extract *24, *40, *120 from description as purchased qty

contradictions.conflict: Rule says purchased qty = case count, but examples only cover retail bottle packs. Wholesale CX 2.5KG*6 (*6 = units-per-case metadata) gets same treatment as 33cl*24 (where *24 IS purchased qty).
vlEvidence: Birra Peroni 33cl*24: same rule correctly yields 24 — asymmetric outcomes from same instruction

sideA.text: Extract ALL invoice line items visible in the table.

sideB.text: Do not hallucinate. / Do NOT extract supplier, total, invoice_date, or invoice_number.

contradictions.conflict: Completeness mandate with no row-count guard or artigo anchor; exclusion rules only cover header/footer metadata fields, not phantom product rows.

sideA.text: Never alter quantity or unit_price to force arithmetic closure.

sideB.text: When quantity is 1, unit_price usually equals the line total column

contradictions.conflict: Post-prompt reconcileLineItemAmounts() fills missing fields via division (not in prompt but downstream). Prompt itself allows semantically wrong complete triples (Ginger Beer) while blocking arithmetic correction.
vlEvidence: Ginger Beer 24×€0.85 with total €19.38 — wrong semantics pass unchecked
contradictions.note: Post-prompt reconcile is outside Pass C prompt scope; included for completeness.

sideA.text: copy each exactly from the invoice

sideB.text: Peito de Frango with quantity column "10" → unit: "kg" (inferred from product type, not column)

contradictions.conflict: Example mixes column qty with name-inferred unit — teaches hybrid authority per field.
vlEvidence: Mammafiore Rulo pass-c-raw: qty=2 kg (unit also inferred from 1kg in name)

summary.primaryConflict: copy-exactly-authoritative (L122) vs infer-from-name (L33-34) with no column-precedence rule
